edge_hypothesis: RSI shallow-dip at 7:1 ACCEPTed with PF=1.333 and an extraordinary transitional PF=5.568. The shallow-dip thesis: RSI 50-55 entries catch brief momentum pauses in an ongoing trend, not deep exhaustion pullbacks. These are higher-probability continuation signals. At 7:1, this already works. But the Supertrend champion (PF=1.921) proves that extreme R:R dramatically improves PF on crypto. This spec tests the shallow-dip signal at 10:1 and 12:1 R:R — keeping only the most explosive continuation moves. Key insight: shallow pullbacks (RSI just below 55) mean the trend is barely pausing, which is exactly when the continuation probability is highest. Combining this with extreme R:R creates a 'momentum slingshot' — enter when momentum briefly dips, hold for massive extension. Invalidation: if PF < 0.9 at 10:1 across 50+ trades, the shallow dip doesn't produce enough extreme continuations to justify the higher R:R.

variants.name: rsi_shallow_slingshot_10to1
variants.description: RSI pullback with shallow-dip entry (RSI <= 55 for long, >= 45 for short) at 10:1 R:R. The shallow RSI threshold captures momentum pauses in strong trends — price hasn't fallen far, just taken a breath. Combined with 10:1 R:R, we only hold winners that extend massively. Stop at 1.0x ATR.
entry_short: RSI_14 >= 45 (shallow pullback from oversold, not recovery).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Exit if EMA_200 trend reverses (close crosses below EMA_200 for longs).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Shallow RSI threshold generates too many signals in choppy trending markets, diluting win rate
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend direction, EMA_50 slope momentum

variants.name: rsi_shallow_slingshot_12to1
variants.description: Same shallow-dip thesis pushed to 12:1 R:R. At 12:1, we need ~9% win rate to break even. Shallow pullbacks in confirmed trends should sustain this. Tests whether the momentum slingshot effect gets stronger at higher R:R by selecting only for the most extreme continuation moves.
entry_short: RSI_14 >= 45 (shallow pullback from oversold, not recovery).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Exit if EMA_200 trend reverses (close crosses below EMA_200 for longs).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 12:1 target on 4h may exceed typical directional leg length, producing almost no winners
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend direction, EMA_50 slope momentum

variants.name: rsi_shallow_slingshot_10to1_1h
variants.description: Shallow-dip 10:1 on 1h timeframe. More frequent entries, smaller ATR stops. Tests whether the momentum slingshot scales to higher frequency — 1h pullbacks are brief, sharp dips that resolve quickly in trending markets.
entry_short: RSI_14 >= 45 (shallow pullback from oversold, not recovery).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 1h noise may cause too many false shallow-dip signals in ranging markets
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend direction, EMA_50 slope momentum
